source_notes.title: Information security manual | Cyber.gov.au
source_notes.excerpt: Information security manual Last updated: March 2026

source_notes.title: November 2023 Essential Eight Maturity Model Update | Cyber.gov.au
source_notes.excerpt: The Essential Eight Maturity Model was last updated on 27 November 2023; ASD updates the E8MM annually. Focus: phishing-resistant MFA, 48-hour critical patching, cloud, logging.

source_notes.title: PSPF Annual Release 2025 | Protective Security Policy Framework
source_notes.excerpt: PSPF 2025 Annual Release embeds Zero Trust, adds new policy on AI, quantum computing and connected peripherals, uplifts Gateway security, requires stocktake of internet-facing systems.

source_notes.title: Security classifications and protective markings | Style Manual (AU Gov)
source_notes.excerpt: The PSPF defines the protective marking scheme: OFFICIAL, OFFICIAL:Sensitive, and security classifications PROTECTED, SECRET, TOP SECRET.

source_notes.title: Policy for the responsible use of AI in government - Version 2.0 | digital.gov.au
source_notes.excerpt: Policy for the responsible use of AI in government Version 2.0 in effect Monday 15 December 2025; v1.1 took effect 1 September 2024. Adds mandatory impact assessments and procurement guidance.

source_notes.title: Guidelines for secure AI system development | NCSC / CISA
source_notes.publisher: UK NCSC & US CISA (+21 international agencies incl. ASD ACSC)
source_notes.excerpt: On 26 November 2023 CISA with UK NCSC published joint Guidelines for Secure AI System Development, developed with 21 other agencies; first of their kind agreed globally; secure-by-design across the AI lifecycle.

source_notes.title: NIST SP 800-218 SSDF v1.1 (Final) | NIST CSRC
source_notes.excerpt: 02/03/22: SP 800-218 (Final). Secure Software Development Framework (SSDF) Version 1.1. A draft Rev.1 (SSDF v1.2) exists in initial public draft form.

source_notes.title: AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) | NIST
source_notes.excerpt: The AI RMF (AI RMF 1.0) was released on 26 January 2023 for voluntary use to incorporate trustworthiness into AI design, development, use and evaluation.

source_notes.title: AI RMF: Generative AI Profile (NIST AI 600-1) | NIST
source_notes.excerpt: NIST AI 600-1, the Generative AI Profile, published 26 July 2024; companion to AI RMF 1.0 defining 12 GenAI risk categories with govern/map/measure/manage actions.

source_notes.title: SLSA - Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts
source_notes.excerpt: SLSA v1.0 released April 2023 by OpenSSF; later specification versions (v1.1/v1.2) published on slsa.dev with progressive build-integrity levels (SLSA 0-3).

source_notes.title: Infosec Registered Assessors Program (IRAP) | Cyber.gov.au
source_notes.excerpt: IRAP, run by ASD, provides independent ICT security assessment against the ISM and PSPF; the program remains active with ongoing assessor enrolment.

source_notes.title: Hosting Certification Framework | Digital Transformation Agency
source_notes.excerpt: The DTA-administered Hosting Certification Framework (replacing the former Cloud Services Certification Program, which ceased 2 March 2020) certifies data-centre/hosting providers (Certified Strategic / Certified Assured) for government workloads.

source_notes.title: SFIA 9 Home | SFIA
source_notes.excerpt: SFIA 9, the global skills and competency framework, published 30 October 2024; refreshes cyber/AI/data skills and expands the catalogue (121 to 147 skills with a health-informatics overlay).

source_notes.title: AI Insights: AI Coding Assistants for developers in HMG | GOV.UK
source_notes.excerpt: Ten principles for AI coding assistants in HMG: main-branch protection with human peer review, secrets separated from AICAs, multi-layered vulnerability scanning, commit only code you understand, code in the open, verify dependency versions.

source_notes.title: CISA & International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI | CISA
source_notes.publisher: US CISA (with ASD ACSC, NSA, FBI, UK/CA/NZ/DE/NL partners)
source_notes.excerpt: Joint guide 'Careful Adoption of Agentic AI Services' from CISA with ASD ACSC and partners: security challenges/risks of agentic AI and recommended mitigations for designers, vendors and operators.

source_notes.title: AGENTS.md - open format for guiding coding agents
source_notes.excerpt: AGENTS.md is an open, vendor-neutral format (60,000+ projects) for the human-authored context coding agents need; stewarded by the Agentic AI Foundation. It is a file convention, not a government security standard.

claims.statement: The ACSC/ASD Information Security Manual (ISM) current release is the March 2026 edition; ASD revises the ISM roughly quarterly.

evidence.quote: Information security manual Last updated: March 2026
This update adopted language from mapped controls within the Information security manual (ISM) to ensure consistency

claims.falsifier: A cyber.gov.au ISM page or OSCAL release dated later than March 2026 at retrieval would falsify 'current'.

claims.statement: The Essential Eight Maturity Model's most recent substantive revision is the 27 November 2023 update (phishing-resistant MFA, 48-hour critical patching, logging/cloud uplift); it remains the current maturity model at retrieval.

evidence.quote: The Essential Eight Maturity Model was last updated on 27 November 2023

claims.falsifier: A cyber.gov.au E8MM 'last updated' date after Nov 2023 would falsify this.

claims.statement: The current PSPF is the 2025 Annual Release (published 24 July 2025), which newly adds policy content on AI, quantum and connected peripherals and embeds Zero Trust.

evidence.quote: PSPF 2025 embeds Zero Trust principles... introduces new policy content on AI, quantum computing, and connected peripheral technologies

claims.falsifier: A protectivesecurity.gov.au 'PSPF Annual Release 2026' superseding the 2025 release at retrieval would falsify 'current'.

claims.statement: The Australian information classification scheme (OFFICIAL, OFFICIAL:Sensitive, PROTECTED, SECRET, TOP SECRET) is defined by the PSPF; agent-enterprise's OFFICIAL/trust tagging aligns with this scheme.

evidence.quote: The PSPF defines the protective marking scheme: OFFICIAL, OFFICIAL:Sensitive, and security classifications PROTECTED, SECRET, TOP SECRET

claims.falsifier: PSPF policy showing the scheme no longer uses OFFICIAL:Sensitive / PROTECTED labels would falsify this.

claims.statement: The DTA Policy for the responsible use of AI in government is at Version 2.0, effective 15 December 2025 (succeeding v1.1 of 1 September 2024), adding mandatory impact assessments and procurement guidance.

evidence.quote: Policy for the responsible use of AI in government - Version 2.0 ... Version 2.0: Comes into effect Monday 15 December 2025

claims.falsifier: A digital.gov.au page showing a version other than 2.0 as current, or a different effective date, would falsify this.

control_anchor.control_id: Policy for the responsible use of AI in government v2.0

claims.statement: The CISA/NCSC 'Guidelines for Secure AI System Development' were published 26 November 2023 with 21+ international partners (including ASD ACSC), establishing secure-by-design across the AI lifecycle.

evidence.quote: On 26 November 2023, the US CISA, together with the UK's NCSC, published joint 'Guidelines for Secure AI System Development' ... in cooperation with 21 other agencies

claims.falsifier: NCSC/CISA pages dating the guidelines other than November 2023 would falsify this.

claims.statement: NIST SP 800-218 (SSDF) current published version is 1.1, finalized 3 February 2022; a Revision 1 (SSDF v1.2) exists only as an initial public draft.

evidence.quote: 02/03/22: SP 800-218 (Final). Secure Software Development Framework (SSDF) Version 1.1

claims.falsifier: A finalized SP 800-218 Rev.1 (v1.2) on csrc.nist.gov would falsify '1.1 is current'.

claims.statement: NIST AI RMF 1.0 was released 26 January 2023; its Generative AI Profile NIST AI 600-1 was published 26 July 2024 with 12 GenAI risk categories.

evidence.quote: The AI RMF was released in January 2023 ... released on January 26, 2023
NIST AI Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile was published on July 26, 2024, with the official designation NIST AI 600-1

claims.falsifier: NIST publishing AI RMF 2.0 or re-dating AI 600-1 would alter these version anchors.

claims.statement: SLSA reached v1.0 in April 2023; the slsa.dev specification has since advanced to later 1.x releases defining progressive build-integrity levels.

evidence.quote: SLSA version 1.0 was released in April 2023 by OpenSSF ... v1.2 documentation

claims.falsifier: slsa.dev/spec showing v1.0 (not 1.1/1.2) as latest stable would falsify the 'advanced' part; the precise current minor was not confirmed from the issuing site.

claims.statement: IRAP (run by ASD, assessing against ISM/PSPF) remains an active assessment program, and government data-centre/hosting assurance is administered via the DTA Hosting Certification Framework (which replaced the Cloud Services Certification Program, ceased 2 March 2020).

evidence.quote: independent security assessment services... against the ISM
the Cloud Services Certification Program (CSCP) ceased on 2 March 2020

claims.falsifier: cyber.gov.au showing IRAP discontinued, or DTA showing the Hosting Certification Framework retired, would falsify this.

claims.statement: SFIA 9 (published 30 October 2024) is the current version of the Skills Framework for the Information Age, expanding to 147 skills and refreshing cyber/AI/data competencies.

evidence.quote: SFIA 9 ... published in October 2024 ... expanding the total number of skills from 121 to 147

claims.falsifier: sfia-online.org announcing SFIA 10 as current would falsify this.

claims.statement: A reusable GOVERNMENT AI-coding governance baseline already exists: the UK GDS/DSIT 'AI Coding Assistants for developers in HMG' (Sept 2025) sets ten reusable principles overlapping agent-enterprise's build-time defense and review controls.

evidence.quote: Merges made to the main branch need to be subject to human peer review ... secrets are separated from AICAs ... multi-layered approach to vulnerability scanning ... You should only commit code changes that you understand

claims.falsifier: If the GOV.UK document were withdrawn or shown to be discussion-only with no reusable principles, the redundancy claim would weaken.

control_anchor.framework: UK GDS AI Coding Assistants Guidance
control_anchor.control_id: Ten principles (branch protection, secrets, scanning, review)

claims.statement: CISA's joint 'Careful Adoption of Agentic AI Services' (late 2025, co-authored by ASD ACSC + Five Eyes) is government-endorsed agentic-AI security guidance, but is adoption/risk guidance, not a packaged build-time agent-governance template like agent-enterprise.

evidence.quote: CISA, in collaboration with the Australian Signals Directorate's Australian Cyber Security Centre (ASD's ACSC) and other partners, released guidance ... 'Careful Adoption of Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) Services'

claims.falsifier: If CISA/ASD shipped an actual deployable agent-governance template or reference repo (not just principles), this 'not a template' framing would be falsified.

control_anchor.control_id: Careful Adoption of Agentic AI Services

claims.statement: No AUSTRALIAN-government (ACSC/DTA) reusable AI-coding-agent build template or 'AGENTS.md for government' standard exists; AU gov publishes policy (DTA AI Policy v2.0) and risk guidance, not a deployable coding-agent governance baseline — leaving a genuine gap agent-enterprise fills, though it overlaps materially with the UK GDS guidance.

evidence.quote: AGENTS.md is a simple, open format for guiding coding agents ... stewarded by the Agentic AI Foundation under the Linux Foundation
ten specific principles tailored to AI coding assistants

claims.falsifier: Discovery of an ACSC/DTA-published, deployable AI-coding-agent template or a 'gov AGENTS.md' baseline repo would falsify the 'no AU gov template' claim outright.
